class.name: HTMLTemplateRenderer
class.brief_description: A class that can parse and render HTML using a simple templating language.
class.description: A class that can parse and render HTML using a simple templating language. This class uses a templating syntax like: [code]{{ EXPRESSION }}[/code]. Everything inbetween [code]{{ }}[/code] is an expression that the system will replace during rendering with actual values from a passed in Dictionary. Available expressions: {{ <expr> }}: Print escaped. Whatever is returned by expr gets printed and html excaped (except if the outer expression is pr, pbr, qpr, qprb, vf, qvf.). Null turns into an empty string. {{; }}: Supress. Runs expression, no output. {{% }} Print raw. Prints the result of the expression as raw unescaped html. Null turns into an empty string. {{if }} ... html ... {{elif }} ... html ... {{else}} ... html ... {{endif}}: if / else / elif / endif. {{for in }} ... html ... {{endfor}}: Foreach. Assuming you pass in the following Dictionary to [method render]: [code]var d : Dictionary = { "var": VALUE, "var1": VALUE, "var2": VALUE, "var_dict": { "x": VALUE }, "var_arr": [ VALUE, ] }[/code] 0. To escape [code]{{[/code] use: [code]{\{[/code]. [code]{\\{[/code] will turn into [code]{\{[/code] etc. 1. [code]p(var)[/code] - print, escaped, also includes to string cast. 2. [code]{{ var }}[/code] - equivalent to [code]p(var)[/code]. 3. [code](var)[/code] - equivalent to [code]p(var)[/code]. 4. [code]pr(var)[/code] - print_raw, not escaped, also includes a cast to string. 5. [code]pb(var)[/code] - print_newline_to_br, escaped, turns newlines into br tags, also includes a to string cast. 6. [code]prb(var)[/code] - print_raw_newline_to_br, not escaped, turns newlines into br tags, also includes a to string cast. 7. [code]vf("%d %d", var1, var2)[/code] - vformat or snprintf or String fromatting using % in gdscript. 8. [code]qp(var1, var2)[/code] - Same as p, but only prints when it's first argument evaluates to true. (First arg is not printed!) 9. [code]qpr(var1, var2)[/code] - Same as pr, but only prints when it's first argument evaluates to true. (First arg is not printed!) 10. [code]qpb(var1, var2)[/code] - Same as pb, but only prints when it's first argument evaluates to true. (First arg is not printed!) 11. [code]qprb(var1, var2)[/code] - Same as prb, but only prints when it's first argument evaluates to true. (First arg is not printed!) 12. [code]qvf(var1, "%d %d", var1, var2)[/code] - Same as vf, but only prints when it's first argument evaluates to true. (First arg is not printed!) 13. [code]p(var_arr[0])[/code] - Array indexing. 14. [code]p(var_dict["x"]), p(var_dict[x]), p(var_dict['x'])[/code] - Dictionary indexing. 15. [code]p(var1, var2)[/code] - All methods supports multiple arguments. 16. Let's say [code]var1[/code] is a [Vector3], [code]var["x"][/code] and [code]var[0][/code] work. 17. Indexing will also call [code]get()[/code] on [Object]s. 18. [code]p(var[var[var[2]]])[/code] Recursive indexing. 19. Method calls on object that are passed in. 20. Array creation: [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ] 21. Dictionary creation: { "key1": "val1", "key2": "val2" } 22. exists("varname"): Returns whether the given variable is set. 23. approx_equals(var1, var2): Returns whether the 2 variables are approximately equal. 24. setvar("varname", value): Sets variable's value in the input dictionary. 25. setvar_if(expr, "varname", value): Sets variable's value in the input dictionary, if expr evaluates to true. 26. getvar("varname"): Returns varname's value or null. 27. getvar("varname", default_value): Returns varname's value, or the default given. 28. range(to): Creates an iterator that goes from 0 to to. 29. range(from, to): Creates an iterator that goes from from to to. 30. range(from, to, step): Creates an iterator that goes from from to to by step. 31. keys(some_dict): Returns the keys from the given dictionary as an array. 32. values(some_dict): Returns the values from the given dictionary as an array. 33. tr("key"): Retrieves a translation from the TranslationServer. 34. trt("key", "locale"): Retrieves a translation from the TranslationServer. 35. select(expr, val1, val2): If expr evaluates to true, then it returns val1, else it returns val2. 36. int(), float(), str(): Conversion methods. 37. Any variant can also be constructed: Vector2, Vector3, Transform ... 38. Methods on variants can also be called: "str".strip_edges() ... So this is how your HTML will look like: [code]... HTML markup ... {{ p(var) }} ... {{ pr(var) }} ... {{ pb(var) }} ... {{ prb(var, var2) }} ... {{ vf("%d %d", var1, var2) }} ... etc.[/code] Note: For a different approach to generating HTML, you can also take a look at [HTMLBuilder]. Note: In the old language this also worked: [code]var[x][/code], where x is supposed to be a string. This doesn't work anymore, as with the current feature set this would be very error prone, and confusing.

method.name: compile
method.description: Compile the given template. Returns true if an error happened.

method.name: get_compile_error_str
method.description: Returns the compile error string (if any).

method.name: render
method.description: Render the template using the data dictionary as it's inputs.
